# opnsense_dnsmasq_boot (Data Source)

Look up an existing Dnsmasq DHCP boot entry on OPNsense by UUID without importing it into Terraform management.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "opnsense_dnsmasq_boot" "example" {
  id = "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012"
}

output "dnsmasq_boot_id" {
  value = data.opnsense_dnsmasq_boot.example.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) UUID to look up.

### Read-Only

- `address` (String) Boot server address.
- `description` (String) Description for this DHCP boot entry.
- `filename` (String) Boot filename served to matching clients.
- `interface` (String) Interface this boot entry applies to; blank means any allowed interface.
- `server_name` (String) Boot server name.
- `tag` (Set of String) Tags this boot entry matches.
